title: Automatic Wire Cutter and Stripper Mechanism
design_question: How to automatically cut and strip insulation from wire to programmable lengths?
selected_approach: Motorised feed roller (NEMA17 stepper, 40 mm rubber roller) advances wire by programmable length. Two solenoid-actuated V-blade assemblies: outer blades cut wire, inner blades (adjustable depth cam stop) score and strip insulation. Arduino Uno controls: stepper steps = length/roller_circumference, solenoid dwell time 200 ms per operation. Blade depth adjusted by 3D-printed cam with 6 pre-set positions for AWG 20–28.
rationale: Solenoid actuation gives reliable, fast (< 200 ms) blade movement. Stepper ensures ±1 mm strip length accuracy. Dual-blade design performs both cut and strip in one station. No servo backlash issues.
verification_notes: FACT: 'Wire Cutting and Insulation Stripping — Blade Mechanics' confirms blade gap = conductor diameter + 0.1 mm clearance.
supporting_fact_titles: Wire Cutting and Insulation Stripping — Blade Mechanics
Stepper Motor Drive — Full Step and Microstepping
